untranslatable_reason: Signal requires measurement of 'Long condition trigger count / Total bars vs Short condition trigger count / Total bars differential' and dual-regime Sharpe ratio calculations (uptrend ≥0.8 AND downtrend ≥0.8). These are meta-level statistical comparisons across regimes and historical performance metrics that cannot be expressed as point-in-time indicator conditions using the allowed operators and indicators. The rule fundamentally requires backtesting infrastructure to compute regime-separated Sharpe ratios and condition trigger frequency analysis, not just real-time indicator comparisons.
translation_notes: This signal is about eliminating directional bias in rule design through statistical symmetry testing, not about trading on specific indicator values. The core intent (requiring balanced long/short trigger frequency <25% differential AND equal performance in both trending regimes) requires custom backtesting logic outside the scope of condition-based rule translation. A proxy using ADX for trend confirmation has been provided, but does not capture the symmetry bias elimination core to ARONSON_DRY_0_2.
